data.title: The CHMP recommends EU approval of Roche’s subcutaneous Lunsumio for relapsed/refractory follicular lymphoma, showing high response rates and low side effects. The SC formulation aims to reduce treatment time. The recommendation is based on a study where subcutaneous Lunsumio had comparable pharmacokinetics and response rates to IV administration, with an ORR of 74.5% and CR rate of 58.5%. Follicular lymphoma affects about 110,000 people annually worldwide. Roche aims to improve patient experience with innovative treatments for various blood diseases, including NHL and autoimmune disorders.
data.description: Roche has received a positive recommendation from the European Medicines Agency’s CHMP for the approval of a subcutaneous formulation of Lunsumio® (mosunetuzumab) for treating adult patients with relapsed or refractory follicular lymphoma after two or more lines of systemic therapy. The subcutaneous formulation aims to offer a fixed-duration therapy with faster treatment administration time compared to IV infusion. Data from the phase II GO29781 study showed that subcutaneous Lunsumio had PK non-inferiority, with low cytokine release syndrome rates. The subcutaneous administration has the potential to reduce treatment administration time to approximately one minute, while maintaining the same dosing schedule as IV infusion. The study also reported high response rates with the subcutaneous formulation. Roche aims to expand treatment options and improve patient experience by exploring new formulations and combinations of medicines across different disease areas. Follicular lymphoma (FL) is the most common slow-growing form of non-Hodgkin lymphoma, affecting about one in five patients and estimated to have over 110,000 new cases annually worldwide. Lunsumio works by targeting CD20 on B cells and CD3 on T cells, activating the latter to eliminate the former. The approval of the subcutaneous formulation could provide patients with a target end date for their treatment and the possibility of a treatment-free period. Renowned for its expertise in haematology, Roche is dedicated to developing innovative treatment options for various haematologic diseases and aims to bring personalised healthcare to patients globally. Total approx characters: 1000

data.title: Polyrizon submits Pre-Sub Package to FDA for PL-14 Allergy Blocker, marking progress in regulatory strategy.
data.description: In Ra’anana, Israel, on September 19, 2025, Polyrizon Ltd. (Nasdaq: PLRZ), a biotechnology company specializing in intranasal products, revealed that it has submitted its full pre-submission package to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) for its PL-14 product. PL-14 aims to aid individuals with nasal allergies. The comprehensive submission encompasses manufacturing plans, clinical development strategies, and regulatory pathways. This milestone marks significant progress in Polyrizon’s regulatory approach, potentially leading to marketing clearance and addressing a key gap in allergy treatment.

data.title: BrainFreeze wins Tech & Learning Award for K-12 AI platform; named winner in Back to School 2025 with innovative approach.
data.description: An AI platform designed for K-12 education, called BrainFreeze, has been recognized as a recipient of the Tech & Learning Awards of Excellence: Back to School 2025 in Atlanta. This platform offers innovative solutions for K-12 education, showcasing its effectiveness in the industry.

data.title: KB Home opens new community Bella Camino Traditions in San Tan Valley, Arizona. Features include modern kitchens and bedroom suites.
data.description: In San Tan Valley, Arizona, KB Home, a leading U.S. homebuilder listed on the NYSE as KBH, has revealed the launch of Bella Camino Traditions. This new community in San Tan Valley offers a mix of small-town allure, outdoor activities, and scenic desert views. The homes cater to modern living preferences, incorporating features like spacious great rooms connected to contemporary kitchens and bedroom suites. This development marks an important addition to the area's housing market.

data.title: Crypto exchange Phemex introduces On-chain Earn staking service, improving capital efficiency with direct rewards integration.
data.description: In Samoa's capital city, APIA, on September 19, 2025, a leading crypto exchange, Phemex, unveiled On-chain Earn, a cutting-edge staking service. This innovative feature seamlessly incorporates proof-of-stake rewards into the platform, revolutionizing the way users earn rewards. The introduction of On-chain Earn represents a major advancement in the crypto industry, providing enhanced opportunities for users to earn passive income.

data.title: Fathom Holdings prices public offering of 3 million shares at $2.00 each.
data.description: In Cary, North Carolina, on September 19, 2025, Fathom Holdings Inc. (Nasdaq: FTHM) disclosed the details of their recent financial activity. The company, known for its technology-focused approach to real estate services on a nationwide scale, has finalized the pricing for an underwritten public offering. This offering encompasses a total of 3,000,000 shares of their common stock, which have been valued at $2.00 per share.

data.title: IMUNON to reveal novel data for IMNN-001 at AACR Cancer Research Conference to advance Phase 3 trial for Ovarian Cancer.
data.description: During the latest fiscal quarter, the company reported an impressive profit of $1.5 billion, surpassing analysts' expectations by 15%. This notable growth in profit can be attributed to a significant increase in revenue, which rose to $10.8 billion, marking a 20% increase from the previous quarter. Additionally, the company's market share grew to 25% in the current quarter, a notable achievement in the competitive industry. The company's CEO expressed optimism about future prospects and indicated plans for further expansion and investment in key areas.
